text.title: WTP Survey Questionnaire Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every WTP Survey Questionnaire application MUST enforce, plus a skip-this-methodology fallback. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Questionnaire MUST include the canonical 4 Van Westendorp questions exactly: 'At what price would you consider the product so expensive you would not buy?', 'cheap enough you would buy?', 'too cheap to trust quality?', 'too expensive but you would still consider?'. Re-phrasings or drops are rejected.
rule.rationale: The four questions are calibrated; re-phrasing breaks the cross-question math that produces the acceptable price range.

rule.statement: Stimulus MUST NOT mention current price, competitor price, or any number. Anchoring contaminates the elicitation.
rule.rationale: Numerical anchors shift WTP estimates 15-30%; the survey then re-discovers the anchor and labels it 'preference'.

rule.statement: Each pricing segment MUST have n >= 200 respondents. Below 200 the optimal-price-point confidence interval is wider than the prices being compared.
rule.rationale: Empirical sample-size analysis of Van Westendorp data: below n=200 the OPP CI ranges +/- 30%, which is wider than typical pricing decisions.

rule.statement: Purchase intent MUST be measured at >=5 price points spanning the Van Westendorp acceptable range. Single-price testing is rejected.
rule.rationale: The price-elasticity curve cannot be inferred from a point estimate; the ladder is the minimum information needed for revenue-optimisation.

rule.statement: Scoring sheet MUST emit: acceptable price range, indifference point, optimal price point, point-of-marginal-cheapness, point-of-marginal-expensiveness, each with 95% CI. Reports without CIs are rejected.
rule.rationale: Pricing decisions made on point estimates without CIs over-fit to noise; the CI determines whether the recommendation is signal or coin-flip.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition / triggered skip, and route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Methodology hygiene: mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
